title: Today's Tech Brief: Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
summary: A look at the push for AI filtering and bioweapons protection
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
imageCaption: Let us filter AI slop, you cowards (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm checking out an article from The Verge, 'Let us filter AI slop, you cowards.' The idea here is that online platforms like Google, YouTube, Meta, Instagram, and TikTok could give users a filter option for AI-generated content. This would allow us to see if AI labels actually work. But, as the article points out, if they did offer this filter, they'd have to face the reality of how much AI-generated content is out there. I mean, can you imagine being able to filter out all the AI-generated noise on social media? It's an interesting thought experiment, and I'm curious to see how this plays out.

AI leaders are calling for tougher protections against AI-aided bioweapons. It's rare that AI industry rivals agree on much, but they're all concerned that their technology could be used to develop biological weapons. This is a big deal, and it's interesting to see the industry coming together on this issue. I guess it's a good thing that they're thinking about the potential risks of their creations.

Another story caught my eye - AI-generated child sexual abuse material is becoming a growing concern. It's a disturbing trend, and I'm not sure how we're going to tackle it. But it's clear that AI can be used for some really bad stuff, and we need to be aware of that.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
